response.justification: This candidate is an excellent full-stack engineer with strong software craftsmanship practices and brief fintech exposure (RIDE Capital), but lacks the mandatory core requirements for the role. They have only ~6 months of C++ experience at National Instruments (not the required 5–10 years), minimal SQL expertise in financial contexts, no Windows platform systems development background, and zero fixed-income or capital-markets domain knowledge. Their entire recent career (2015–present) has been in web/full-stack technologies (TypeScript, Node.js, React), making them a poor fit despite genuine engineering talent.
strengths: Strong communication and mentoring skills demonstrated through workshops, conference talks, and team leadership across multiple organizations
Proven software craftsmanship with TDD, CI/CD pipeline management, and disciplined development practices
Some fintech exposure through RIDE Capital role, showing willingness to work in financial services
concerns: Severely insufficient C++ experience (~6 months vs. mandatory 5–10 years); last C++ work was in 2015 on LabWindows components
Technology stack completely misaligned: last 10+ years focused on TypeScript, Node.js, and web development rather than systems programming or Windows platforms
No fixed-income, capital-markets, investment-banking, or financial-products domain expertise; fintech exposure (RIDE Capital) was through web stack, not backend systems
